story_clusters.summary: During Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Abu Dhabi, India and the UAE finalized agreements worth $5 billion, including energy, defense, and shipping pacts. Additionally, the UAE will store up to 30 million barrels of oil in India’s strategic reserves as part of the energy cooperation outlined in the deals.

story_clusters.summary: During the BRICS foreign ministers meeting, India’s Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with Iran’s Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian and Russia’s Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov, marking their first engagement since regional tensions in West Asia. Iran’s Foreign Minister emphasized that India could play a greater role in promoting peace in West Asia, while India’s External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar stressed the importance of dialogue amid global uncertainty. The discussions occurred amid broader efforts to address regional conflicts in West Asia.

story_clusters.summary: State-run oil marketing companies (OMCs) in India increased petrol and diesel prices by ₹3 per litre, citing mounting losses and a West Asia crisis. The price hike was implemented in four major metros and followed closely after state elections. Analysts question whether the ₹3 increase will sufficiently offset the OMCs' financial strain.

story_clusters.summary: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar and Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov discussed trade, sustainable currency mechanisms, and logistics during their meeting, according to the Russian Embassy. Lavrov also stated that he and Prime Minister Narendra Modi, along with Jaishankar, discussed increased Russian energy supplies to India. Additionally, Jaishankar criticized unilateral sanctions ahead of the expiration of the U.S. waiver on Russian oil imports.

story_clusters.summary: India failed to secure a consensus at the BRICS foreign ministers' meeting due to disagreements over the Israel-Hamas conflict and Red Sea tensions, with Iran dissenting on the Palestine issue. The UAE and Iran’s ongoing rift further complicated efforts to issue a joint statement, leaving the bloc divided on West Asia. Professor Hamed Mousavi of the University of Tehran also called for BRICS to mediate in the Iran-US conflict.

Jaishankar and Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister Ali Bagheri Araghchi discussed the Iran crisis and bilateral issues at the BRICS FMM. Separately, tensions escalated near the Strait of Hormuz as one ship was seized and another sunk amid ongoing US-Iran hostilities in West Asia.

articles.title: Karnataka HC Upholds GST Bank Attachment as No Prior Section 83 Hearing Is Required
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court held that Section 83 of the CGST Act does not mandate a pre-decisional hearing before provisional attachment of bank accounts. The Court ruled that statutory safeguards are available through post-decisional remedies under Rule 159(5). The post Karnataka HC Upholds GST Bank Attachment as No Prior Section 83 Hearing Is Required appeared first on TaxGuru .
articles.raw_summary: The Karnataka High Court held that Section 83 of the CGST Act does not mandate a pre-decisional hearing before provisional attachment of bank accounts. The Court ruled that statutory safeguards are available through post-decisional remedies under Rule 159(5). The post Karnataka HC Upholds GST Bank Attachment as No Prior Section 83 Hearing Is Required appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Bombay HC Quashes GST ITC Blocking as Restriction Continued Beyond One Year
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court held that blocking of Input Tax Credit under Rule 86A automatically ceases after one year. The Court ruled that continued restriction beyond the statutory period was illegal and arbitrary. The post Bombay HC Quashes GST ITC Blocking as Restriction Continued Beyond One Year appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: SC Upholds Reassessment Quash Order Due to Mere Suspicion Without ‘Reason to Believe’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court (SC) dismissed the Revenue Department's challenge against a Calcutta High Court (HC) order that quashed reassessment proceedings under Section 147 of the Income Tax Act. articles.title: SC reiterates IBC can’t be used as tool for coercion, debt recovery in breach of contract cases
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India reiterated that the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) cannot be used as a tool for coercion or debt recovery in cases that are primarily contractual disputes. The court upheld a ruling by the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT), emphasizing that insolvency proceedings should not be invoked for breaches of contract. This decision reinforces the original intent of the IBC to address genuine insolvency situations rather than commercial disagreements.

articles.title: Failure to Appoint CS for Six Years: MCA imposes Penalty on 9 Directors & Company
articles.summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) penalized a company and nine of its directors for failing to appoint a whole-time Company Secretary from 2014 to 2020, violating legal requirements under Section 203(5) of the Companies Act. The Registrar of Companies found the non-compliance and imposed penalties as a regulatory enforcement measure. This highlights the importance of corporate governance and compliance with statutory appointments in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Registrar of Companies found that the company remained without a whole-time Company Secretary from 2014 to 2020 in violation of mandatory legal requirements. articles.title: CESTAT Deletes Service Tax Demand Because Photo Printing Activity Was Held as Manufacture
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) ruled that converting digital photographs into printed products qualifies as 'manufacture' rather than a taxable photography service. This decision led to the deletion of a service tax demand against the taxpayer. The ruling clarifies the tax treatment of photo printing activities under India's service tax laws.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that conversion of digital photographs into printed photo products created a commercially distinct commodity, qualifying as manufacture rather than taxable photography service. articles.title: US, China discussing AI 'guardrails': Treasury secretary Bessent
articles.summary: The United States and China are discussing setting up 'guardrails' for the use of artificial intelligence (AI), according to US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. The two countries aim to establish a protocol to ensure non-state actors do not gain access to advanced AI models. This comes amid growing global calls for regulation of AI technology.
articles.raw_summary: The United States and China were discussing setting up "guardrails" for the use of artificial intelligence (AI), US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told CNBC on Thursday, with growing calls for regulation of the burgeoning technology. The Treasury secretary said Washington and Beijing would set up a "protocol" on the path forward on AI, particularly "to make sure non-state actors don't get a hold of these models."

articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court ruled that the disputed Bhojshala site in Dhar is a Saraswati temple and directed the Centre and Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) to manage its affairs. The Hindu side had previously demanded exclusive rights to worship at the site. The ruling has implications for religious rights and governance of disputed sites in India.

The ruling provides clarity on how past demonetization-related transactions should be treated under current tax laws.

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Raipur ruled that tax assessments based on server data, search records, and investigation reports must be quashed if such material is not provided to the taxpayer. The Tribunal held that denying the assessee an opportunity to rebut the evidence violates principles of natural justice. This decision underscores the importance of procedural fairness in tax administration and could influence future tax policy and litigation.

The court's intervention suggests a legal or regulatory dispute involving Apple is pending resolution. The CCI is a key regulatory body in India overseeing competition matters.
articles.raw_summary: Don’t pass final order against Apple till July 15: HC to CCI

articles.title: Govt imposes Rs 3/litre windfall tax on petrol exports, cuts diesel and ATF export levies
articles.summary: The Indian government has imposed a Rs 3 per litre windfall tax on petrol exports while reducing levies on diesel and aviation turbine fuel (ATF) exports. Effective May 16, these changes aim to ensure domestic fuel availability and curb excessive profits amid high global crude oil prices due to the West Asia conflict. The move targets petrol, diesel, and ATF, which are critical for India's energy security and trade balance.
articles.raw_summary: The government has imposed a Rs 3 per litre windfall tax on petrol exports while reducing levies on diesel and aviation fuel. These revised rates, effective May 16, aim to ensure domestic fuel availability and prevent excessive profits from elevated global crude oil prices amid the West Asia conflict.

articles.title: Maharashtra stops bike rallies, cuts ministers’ car convoys by half
articles.summary: The Maharashtra government has announced a 19-point austerity plan to reduce fuel consumption and costs. Key measures include banning bike rallies, halving ministerial car convoys, promoting electric vehicles, and restricting foreign trips. The plan aims to save expenses and conserve resources amid economic pressures.
articles.raw_summary: Maharashtra has announced a 19-point austerity plan that bans bike rallies, cuts ministerial convoys, pushes EV use and restricts foreign trips to save fuel and costs.

articles.title: TDB kicks off RDI fund disbursal, backs startups across space, drones, biotech
articles.summary: The Technology Development Board (TDB) has begun disbursing the first tranche of the Rs 1 lakh crore Research and Development Initiative (RDI) fund, backing deeptech startups in space, drones, and biotech. The board expects to disburse Rs 500-600 crore this fiscal year, with initial investments ranging from Rs 3 crore to Rs 150 crore per project.

articles.title: Co-Owner Is 'Landlord' Under Bombay Rent Act, Can Maintain Eviction Suit Without Exclusive Title: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has held that a co-owner of a property qualifies as a “landlord” under the Bombay Rents, Hotel and Lodging House Rates Control Act, 1947, and is therefore entitled to maintain eviction proceedings even without exclusive title or formal partition of the tenanted premises. A bench of Justice Manoj Misra and Justice Manmohan set aside the Bombay High Court's decision that had overturned the concurrent findings of the trial court and the first appellate court in an eviction dispute

These promises could shape future agricultural policies if implemented.

articles.title: ITAT Grants Section 12AB Registration as Missing PAN Details Cannot Make Small Donations Non-Genuine
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Nagpur ruled that small donations received by an organization cannot be deemed non-genuine solely due to missing PAN or donor address details. The tribunal granted Section 12AB registration, affirming that nominal contributions do not invalidate the organization's activities or eligibility for tax exemptions. No evidence was found to question the genuineness of the donations or the organization's operations.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Nagpur held that nominal donations received in small amounts could not be treated as non-voluntary contributions merely because PAN or full address details of donors were unavailable. The Tribunal found no adverse evidence questioning the genuineness of donations or activities. The post ITAT Grants Section 12AB Registration as Missing PAN Details Cannot Make Small Donations Non-Genuine appeared first on TaxGuru .

This decision impacts how tax authorities can reopen past assessments and sets a precedent for future tax disputes.

articles.title: ITAT Deletes Addition as WhatsApp Chats Lacked Section 65B Certification
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) deleted an addition to a taxpayer's alleged undisclosed income because the department relied solely on uncertified WhatsApp chats as evidence. The tribunal ruled that such digital evidence must comply with Section 65B of the Indian Evidence Act, and the department failed to prove any actual transaction involving the assessee. This decision underscores the legal requirement for proper certification of electronic records in tax proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that addition of alleged undisclosed income could not be sustained merely on the basis of WhatsApp chats without supporting enquiry or evidence. It held that the department failed to establish any actual transaction involving the assessee. The post ITAT Deletes Addition as WhatsApp Chats Lacked Section 65B Certification appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Sugar export ban to free up 4-5 lakh tonne for domestic use
articles.summary: India has banned sugar exports until September 30. This move aims to increase domestic availability by 4-5 lakh tonnes. The decision is a response to El Nino concerns and potential impacts on future production. While boosting buffer stocks, the ban may affect sugar mill owners' short-term finances. Overseas demand had recently surged, but this ban redirects those supplies inward.

articles.title: GSTAT की नई व्यवस्था: डिवीजन बेंच, सिंगल बेंच और अपीलों के वर्गीकरण की विस्तृत व्याख्या
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) has issued a new order stating that all pending and new appeals will first be listed before a Division Bench. Cases will only be referred to a Single Bench if there is no question of law involved. This change aims to streamline the adjudication process for GST-related disputes.

articles.title: India’s cities need Rs 80 lakh crore investment to support urban growth: Report
articles.summary: India requires Rs 80 lakh crore for urban infrastructure by 2037. Cities will contribute significantly to the nation's GDP. A new Rs 1 lakh crore fund will encourage market-based funding for urban projects. Local bodies must raise funds through bonds or loans. This will improve financial discipline and city creditworthiness. Smaller towns can benefit from a credit guarantee scheme.

articles.title: Fuel supply delays hit BPCL, HPCL outlets amid panic buying fears
articles.summary: Fuel supplies from Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) have been delayed by two to three days in some cases over the past three weeks. articles.summary: State-run oil marketing companies (OMCs) increased petrol and diesel prices by about ₹3 per litre, ending a two-year price freeze. The move follows rising crude oil prices, which have led to financial losses for the firms. The price hike could impact inflation and consumer spending, potentially prompting government or regulatory responses.
articles.raw_summary: After over two years of holding prices steady, state-run oil firms raised petrol and diesel prices by about ₹3 a litre, citing mounting under-recoveries amid elevated crude oil prices.

articles.title: Centre raises MSP for 14 kharif crops, boosts support for pulses and oilseeds
articles.summary: The Indian government has increased the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for 14 kharif crops, with the highest hike of 61% for moong. The MSP for paddy (rice) has been raised by Rs 72 per quintal to Rs 2,441 for the common variety. This decision aims to support farmers, particularly for pulses and oilseeds, and is based on expected margins over production costs.
articles.raw_summary: Based on expected margins over cost of production, the MSP hike is the most for moong at 61%, followed by bajra and maize (56% each) and tur (54%). For other crops, it is estimated to be around 50%. The MSP for paddy (rice), the main kharif crop, has been increased by Rs 72 per quintal, taking it to Rs 2,441 for the 'common' variety and Rs 2,461 for 'Grade A'.

articles.title: Fuel hike not enough to offset all losses, oil stocks fall further

Persistent geopolitical tensions continue to support high oil prices, posing challenges for company profitability.

articles.title: Oil casts a shadow on WPI inflation
articles.summary: The article highlights concerns about India's Wholesale Price Index (WPI) inflation being artificially low due to oil prices, which could mislead monetary policy decisions. Since the Consumer Price Index (CPI) guides monetary policy, the distortion in WPI may send incorrect signals about growth-inflation dynamics. The editorial suggests this could lead to misguided policy responses.
articles.raw_summary: Because CPI is the guiding anchor for monetary policy, the artificially low number sends the wrong signal regarding growth-inflation dynamics

articles.title: After Operation Sindoor, all eyes on the BrahMos missile
articles.summary: The article, authored by a doctoral candidate from JNU, examines India's Operation Sindoor and the BrahMos missile system. It highlights the strategic significance of these defence initiatives and their potential impact on India's security policies and international relations. The focus is on the broader implications of these military developments rather than immediate policy changes.

articles.title: Effect of hotter summers on dengue proliferation
articles.summary: The article, authored by Dr. Melih Küreç, examines how rising temperatures due to hotter summers may contribute to the spread of dengue fever. It highlights the medical and public health challenges posed by climate change, particularly in tropical regions like India. The piece underscores the need for proactive measures to mitigate health risks associated with vector-borne diseases.

articles.title: CM Vijay’s oath ceremony sparks CPI, DMK outrage over Thamizhthai Vaazhthu: What is the controversy about?
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's new Chief Minister Vijay was sworn in, promising a secular government. However, the CPI and DMK criticized the order of the state song 'Tamil Thaai Vaazhthu' during the ceremony, demanding it be prioritized in all future government events. The controversy centers on the song's placement and its symbolic importance in state functions. The opposition parties argue this reflects a deviation from secular governance.
articles.raw_summary: Vijay was sworn in as Tamil Nadu's Chief Minister, promising a ‘new era’ of secularism. However, CPI and DMK criticised the order of state song Tamil Thaai Vaazhthu during the ceremony, calling for it to be prioritised in future government events.

articles.title: Explainer-Why India's Tata Sons is facing pressure to list
articles.summary: Until now, Tata Sons has remained unlisted. But there is now pressure to list from internal stakeholders, including its second largest shareholder, the Shapoorji Paloonji (SP) Group. Rules from the Reserve Bank of India may also require them to list unless they can secure an exemption.

articles.title: What is Current Account Deficit? Why PM Modi’s import warning could affect your EMIs and bills
articles.summary: Why is PM Modi warning Indians about imports, fuel use and foreign travel? The answer lies in India’s current account deficit — a key economic indicator that could influence fuel prices, EMIs, inflation and the rupee amid rising global oil prices.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Behind India's abrupt sugar export ban, a worry over sufficient stocks

articles.title: ETtech Explainer: Did OpenAI's new AI deployment venture just kill Indian IT stocks?
articles.summary: Indian IT stocks plummeted to three-year lows following OpenAI's launch of a new venture focused on direct AI implementation for enterprises. This move, alongside a similar initiative by Anthropic, threatens the traditional service integration model of Indian IT giants. The market reaction highlights concerns over AI's disruptive potential and existing geopolitical uncertainties impacting the sector.
